title: potentilla subjuga
intro: Potentilla subjuga, also known as Five-fingered Cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its distinctive five-lobed leaves. The plant is rich in flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Potentilla subjuga include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety require further scientific study.
